source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, $AD$ is the median of $\triangle ABC$. It is known that the perimeter of $\triangle ABD$ is $25\,\text{cm}$, and $AB$ is $7\,\text{cm}$ longer than $AC$. What is the perimeter of $\triangle ACD$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $AD$ is the median of $\triangle ABC$,\\
it follows that $BD=CD$,\\
therefore, the difference in the perimeters of $\triangle ABD$ and $\triangle ACD$ is $(AB+BD+AD)-(AC+CD+AD)=AB-AC$.\\
Since the perimeter of $\triangle ABD$ is $25\,cm$ and $AB$ is $7\,cm$ longer than $AC$,\\
the perimeter of $\triangle ACD$ is: $25-7=\boxed{18}\,(cm)$.

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'As shown in the figure', so the diagram is part of the presentation.
Core solvable fact is stated in text: 'AD is the median of triangle ABC', which already implies BD = DC without needing visual measurement.
No angle, length, shape, or spatial relation appears to require reading hidden information from the image; the figure mainly anchors labels.
Metadata says requires_image=true and text_dominant=false, so image reliance should not be treated as negligible.
multi_step: Requires using the definition of median to infer BD = DC.
Requires relating the two triangle perimeters: P(ACD) = AC + AD + DC and P(ABD) = AB + AD + BD.
Requires combining the condition AB is 7 cm longer than AC to substitute AC = AB - 7, then comparing perimeters.
This is short but clearly more than a one-step lookup.
verifiability: Target answer is a single numeric perimeter value, making final-output checking straightforward.
The reasoning can be verified symbolically from the stated conditions: P(ACD) = P(ABD) - 7 = 18.
Provided answer text is explicit ('18 cm'), and asset registry passed successfully.
The problem has little ambiguity in what constitutes a correct answer.
